title: inula subauriculata
intro: Inula subauriculata, also known as the Korean elecampane, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Inula subauriculata has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits.
